summary: Fan-in agent verified the bridge_built observation chain end-to-end across both new paths. Zero code changes (verification-first design — no glue line was needed). 0 LOC of 50 cap used, 0 files of 2 cap used. Path A (world-map → click-place) and Path B (edge-walk → drag-place) both PASS — they converge on the same ConstructionSystem.tryPlace → completeBuild → dialogEvent (step: activeQuest) → LocalSceneBase.advanceFromDialog → advanceQuest pipeline. Drag mode (commit f647de8) preserves the completion contract bit-identical with click mode. Phaser DryWash pod CLOSES.
review_method: Native code-quality-reviewer dispatch SKIPPED per session-scoped protocol. Orchestrator-completed verification: confirmed receipt landed at expected path, git status clean (only the receipt file as untracked, no rogue code edits), traced event chain in receipt's notes is internally consistent and matches the actual file contents from prior dispatches.

low.summary: Worker correctly identified that questSystem.js:199-202 treats observe-step advancement as automatic — the dialog-tagged step IS the detection signal, and state.observations is informational/journaling rather than the gating mechanism. This explains why no glue line was needed: the 'observation chain' is really a 'completion-dialog-with-step-tag' chain, and the drag path emits the same dialog payload as the click path. Worth noting for future quest-glue dispatches that the observe-step pattern is dialog-driven, not observation-array-gated.

Worker did not re-run npm run build (no edits made). Defensible — build was verified clean in commit f647de8 just minutes prior, and the agent made zero code changes. Build verification is for changes; verification-only agents legitimately skip it.

Receipt notes reference DryWashScene.js:458 for `system.completeBuild()` invocation. This is the bike-crossing tween's onComplete callback per phaser-bridge-construction's drag-mode notes. Worker correctly identified that autoCompleteAfterAllPlaced:false routes through the scene's onAllPlaced rather than auto-firing — so the 'load test' animation finishes before the dialog/observation, identical to commit 451e7a4 behavior.
